journal_entry.description: Construction of fencing, streetlights, walkways, and permanent landscaping for factory
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, land improvements are classified based on their useful life. Permanent landscaping (trees, shrubs, grading) has an indefinite life and is capitalized to Land or Land Improvements (non-depreciable). Fencing, streetlights, and walkways have finite useful lives and are capitalized as depreciable Land Improvements or Property Improvements. The transaction specifies two distinct payments for different types of improvements, requiring two asset accounts. Cash and bank (cheque) are both asset decreases. No tax is mentioned in the transaction, and construction/landscaping services may be taxable, but the amounts stated appear to be final amounts paid, so we record as stated.

output.reason: Two distinct asset increases: (1) Fencing, streetlights, and walkways are depreciable improvements/structures related to the factory = asset increase; (2) Permanent landscaping installations are non-depreciable land improvements = asset increase. These must use distinct accounts per system rules (non-depreciable vs depreciable), but both are asset increases on the debit side. Total: 2 asset increase lines.

debit_classifier.reason: Two distinct asset increases: (1) Fencing, streetlights, and walkways are depreciable improvements/structures related to the factory = asset increase; (2) Permanent landscaping installations are non-depreciable land improvements = asset increase. These must use distinct accounts per system rules (non-depreciable vs depreciable), but both are asset increases on the debit side. Total: 2 asset increase lines.

output.reason: Two asset decreases: (1) Cash paid $1,750,000 for fencing, streetlights, and walkways = asset decrease; (2) Cash (via cheque) paid $2,360,000 for permanent landscaping = asset decrease. Both payments reduce cash/bank assets. The items purchased (infrastructure improvements and permanent landscaping) are capitalized as separate assets on the debit side, but on the credit side we have two distinct payment methods reducing liquid assets.

credit_classifier.reason: Two asset decreases: (1) Cash paid $1,750,000 for fencing, streetlights, and walkways = asset decrease; (2) Cash (via cheque) paid $2,360,000 for permanent landscaping = asset decrease. Both payments reduce cash/bank assets. The items purchased (infrastructure improvements and permanent landscaping) are capitalized as separate assets on the debit side, but on the credit side we have two distinct payment methods reducing liquid assets.
